issues: The Zod schema in parseRefreshTokenResponse uses z.string().toString() which evaluates to the literal string key "ZodString", not a dynamic catch-all. The schema validates nothing useful beyond access_token and should use z.object({ access_token: z.string() }).passthrough() instead.

parseRefreshTokenResponse silently injects the literal string "refresh_token" as the token value when no refresh token is present. This sentinel value will break on the next refresh cycle when used against the OAuth provider.
The webhook endpoint in app-credential.ts uses plain string comparison (!==) for secret validation instead of crypto.timingSafeEqual(), making it vulnerable to timing attacks.

The webhook endpoint passes an empty string fallback to symmetricDecrypt when the CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var is missing, which will either throw a cryptic error or succeed with a weak/empty key. It should fail explicitly.

refreshOAuthTokens adds indirection without removing complexity — callers still need to wrap refresh logic in a lambda, pass appSlug as a magic string, know about credential.userId, and handle response normalization differently.

APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED evaluates to string | undefined rather than boolean, meaning it could leak the actual secret string into a constant imported everywhere. It should be wrapped in Boolean() or !!.

parseRefreshTokenResponse is applied inconsistently — used in Google Calendar, Office365 Calendar, Zoom, and Salesforce but not in HubSpot, Lark, Webex, Zoho Bigin, or Zoho CRM, with no documentation explaining why.
Salesforce gets completely different treatment with a manual fetch + parseRefreshTokenResponse block instead of using refreshOAuthTokens like every other integration.

Office365 Calendar error handling regresses — the old zod error logging was removed, and the control flow between parseRefreshTokenResponse throwing on failure and the caller checking tokenResponse.success is confused.
The webhook handler in app-credential.ts has an empty JSDoc comment (/** */) that is just noise.
The webhook handler does not verify req.method === "POST", so any HTTP method will be accepted.
zoho-bigin/api/add.ts changed from a dynamic slug (appConfig.slug) to a hardcoded "zoho-bigin" string, an undocumented behavioral change mixed into a refactoring PR.

File reorganization moving encodeOAuthState, decodeOAuthState, and createOAuthAppCredential into an oauth/ subdirectory touches ~20 files for what amounts to a folder rename, adding churn for minimal benefit.
